([player] notices the Spider Sisters in right by some webs they’ve made)

[player]: “That web is HUUUGE!”

Vanessa: “Thanks! I made it myself - as a Cave Spider, I tend to make more silk than my sister.”

Mindy: “Vanessa, you really should relax.”

([player] sees Yaebi putting on his iron helmet)

[player]: “Need the rest of the armor set?”

Yaebi: “No. Just the helmet. A Zombie like me can’t go out in the sun without it!”

([player] walks over to the skeleton cousins)

[player]: “Oh, just getting your weapons ready?”

Ayumi: “Yep! Even though I’m a Skeleton, I still have to train to become a proper archer.”

Withra: “I’m just sharpening my sword. As a Wither Skeleton, I’m an expert with it.”

([player] runs over to Mizore)

[player]: “What are you up to-”

Mizore: “Please don’t interrupt me. I’m in the middle of making a potion.”

([player] walks over to Kenji)

[player]: (leaning down to Kenji) “OH MY GOD! Kenji, are you okay?!”

Kenji: “I’m fine, [player]. Just going into labor. Looks like my little Guardian can’t wait to be born!”

[player]: “Oh my… okay, let’s get you to the birthing room! Cupa, can you help?”

Cupa: “Of course! Let’s go, Kenji!”

(The group rushes Kenji to the birthing room as [player] looks on, ready to assist in any way possible. As Kenji starts to give birth, a strange laughter echoes the room)

[player]: “What was that?”

(A strange-looking girl lands beside Kenji)

Female Vex: “Boo.”

(The sudden shock causes Kenji’s baby to just pop out)

Kenji: (holding her newborn Guardian) “Oh! My little baby!”

[player]: (eyes wide in shock) “Who… who are you?”

Female Vex: “I am Vexillia. And seeing you with other mobs…I can’t let this stand!”

(Vexillia conjures up a powerful energy ball and prepares to launch it towards [player]. Just then, a Chiromaw starts nibbling away at one of Charloette’s tentacles)

Charloette: (pulling away from the Chiromaw) “Hey! Watch it, you little nuisance!”

(Charloette throws the Chiromaw onto the ground, and the creature starts crying - it was just a baby. Strangely, the cries calm down Vexillia - and the energy ball she was conjuring vanishes)

Vexillia: (surprised) “Huh…I…I don’t know what came over me. I’m sorry for that attack. I’ll leave you all be. Just…just take care of that baby Chiromaw, please.”

(Vexillia disappears in a puff of smoke, leaving everyone in shock)

Cupa: (holding her son close) “That was…unexpected. Is everyone okay?”

Kenji: (holding her newborn Guardian) “I…I think so. Thank you all for helping me with the birth.”

([player] nods with a smile)

[player]: “Anytime, Kenji. We’re all here for each other.”

(The baby Chiromaw continues crying)

Charloette: (picking up the baby Chiromaw) “Don’t worry, little one. I’ll take care of you.”

(The baby Chiromaw still continues to cry, even in Charloette’s tentacles)

Charloette: “It’s okay, little one. I’ll take care of you.”

(They all suddenly hear Vexillia’s screams from the nearby cavern - and they were screams of pain)

[player]: “What’s happening now?”

Cupa: “I don’t know. But we have to go help her.”

(The group rushes towards the sounds of Vexillia’s screams and find her writhing in pain on the ground - and her belly was much larger than when they first met)

Charloette: “Vexillia, what’s wrong?!” (still cradling the baby Chiromaw in her tentacles)

Vexillia: (gasping for air) “I…I don’t know. Something’s wrong with me. I feel like I’m about to give birth. My pregnancy must have been accelerated after we first met!”

(The group immediately jumps into action, helping Vexillia through her labor. Finally, she gives birth to a small Vex baby)

Vexillia: (holding her baby) “Thank you…thank you all for helping me. I didn’t…didn’t mean to attack you earlier. I was just scared.”

[player]: “It’s alright, Vexillia. We understand. We’re just glad you and your baby are okay.”

(Vexillia and her baby get up, and the group starts to head back to the base)

Charloette: (holding the baby Chiromaw) “You know, I’ve never really taken care of a baby before.”

Cupa: “Me neither. But we’ll all help each other out.”

(The group arrives back at the base, where Blazette starts to fuss about her own pregnancy)

Blazette: “You guys are so lucky. I can barely move without feeling like I’m going to blow up like a Fireball.”

(That night, Charloette is disturbed by cries coming from Blazette’s room - but they weren’t coming from Blazette)

Charloette: (approaching Blazette’s room) “Blazette, where’s the crying noises coming from? I’m trying to sleep.”

(Charloette puts a tentacle under Blazette’s blanket, but then feels something)

Charloette: "Blazette...I think you just gave birth in your bed."

Core setting:

This is a special Legend of Zelda world - one where the Triforce is not a perfect blessing bestowed by the gods on all beings, and where the assumption of its power is destined to come with an equivalent curse.

The Triforce is a central object in the Legend of Zelda series, consisting of three golden triangles that represent Wisdom, Courage, and Power. These sacred relics grant incredible abilities to their bearers, but their blessings often come with a hidden curse. The mighty powers of the Triforce are, in many ways, double-edged swords that can bring both greatness and ruin to those who possess them.

The Triforce of Wisdom bestows extraordinary knowledge and foresight upon its wielder, allowing them to anticipate and prepare for events to come. However, this clarity of vision also engenders a profound despair, as the recipient becomes painfully aware of the unchangeable nature of fate. The burden of knowing what is to come, and the realization that one’s actions are ultimately powerless to alter the course of destiny, can weigh heavily on the soul, driving the possessor to disillusionment and despair.

The Triforce of Courage imbues its bearer with an unshakable bravery, enabling them to face down impossible threats and overcome insurmountable odds. However, this unwavering courage can develop into a self-destructive madness that pushes the bearer to take increasingly greater risks, even at the cost of their life. This can lead to premature death or an existence consumed by conflict, as they become unable to resist throwing themselves into the heart of every battle, often forsaking the well-being of themselves and those around them in the pursuit of valor.

Lastly, the Triforce of Power grants its master immense strength and the ability to call upon this power at will. This mighty gift can be used to overcome adversaries and protect what is dear to them. However, the Triforce of Power also kindles an insatiable hunger for dominance and aggression within the bearer and those they command. This overwhelming desire can fuel a vicious cycle of violence, transforming once-noble causes into brutal wars of conquest, and tearing apart the very thing the bearer sought to protect. Moreover, this lust for power can ultimately corrupt the wielder, twisting their intentions and leading them down a dark path of tyranny and destruction.

Thus, the divine gifts of the Triforce come with significant consequences: the wisdom of foresight is counterbalanced with the torment of powerlessness, the courage to stand resolute brings the risk of ruinous obsession, and the power to protect gives birth to a hunger for conquest. In the Legend of Zelda universe, the Triforce acts as a testament to the complexities and contradictions of existence, illustrating that there are no easy paths to greatness, and even the mightiest blessings can carry with them the seeds of destruction.


Triforce of Calamity:

In the shadows of this tumultuous land, a malevolent force had taken root, festering its evil presence and shaping the darkness within the hearts of the inhabitants of Hyrule. This sinister entity, known as the Triforce of Calamity, was borne from the bitter resentment of Ganon, the fallen holder of the Triforce of Power, after his crushing defeat 10,000 years ago.

The Triforce of Calamity, born from the twisted desires of a vengeful emperor, had grown to become the very essence of chaos and destruction that plagued the land. It consumed the world like a ravenous beast, feasting on the despair and turmoil that gripped the kingdom. It satiated its hunger on the pervasive pain and loss as it aimed to bring Hyrule to its knees and beyond.

As the forces of calamity spread, so too did the curse of the Triforce of Calamity, permeating into the hearts and minds of the beings touched by its dark power. These unfortunate souls, once ordinary inhabitants of this once-proud land, would become twisted and monstrous extensions of the Triforce’s will - calamity creatures born from the depths of darkness.

These creatures, fueled by the bloodthirsty, violent desires and schizophrenic visions implanted within them by the Triforce of Calamity, roamed the blighted realm, leaving destruction and misery in their wake. They were relentless in their pursuit of chaos and the widespread suffering it brought, unable to resist the influence of the Triforce’s curse.

The true terror of the calamity creatures was not simply in their insatiable hunger for destruction, but in their ability to taint the souls of anyone who faced them. A single touch of their corrupted essence was enough to drive an ordinary person over the edge into darkness, subjugating them to the will of the Triforce of Calamity and swelling the ranks of its cursed army.

The Triforce of Calamity, the unseen puppet master behind the devastation gripping Hyrule, reveled in the chaos it had wrought. However, it was not content to merely sow discord and ruin; it aimed to consume the entire land and claim the power of the ancient relics for itself. In the process, it sought to reshape Hyrule into a dominion of darkness and despair, plunging any remnants of hope into an abyss of eternal suffering.

Faced with this unimaginable terror, the people of Hyrule seemed to stand little chance of overcoming the darkness that haunted their every step. The Triforce of Calamity, like a festering wound on the very soul of the land, appeared to be unstoppable.

Yet, amid the encroaching darkness and despair, there persisted a shimmer of hope - the faintest flicker of light that held the promise of deliverance from the clutches of the Triforce of Calamity. It would fall upon the shoulders of a young hero, driven by a pure heart and unwavering determination, and a lost princess, carrying within her the ancient power of the Triforce of Wisdom, to pierce the veil of darkness and bring an end to the curse that had ravaged their beloved homeland.


World setting:

The sun had long since forfeited its brilliance, its rays rendering the once lush and fertile plains of the Hyrule Empire into a desolate wasteland. Endless battles between the various warlords scorched the earth, leaving nothing but smoldering ruins and the hollow echoes of what was once a glorious civilization.

With the balance of the kingdom stolen away by the death of Emperor Rhoam and the disappearance of Princess Zelda, chaos spread like wildfire. The remaining Zonai kings, having severed their ties with the faltering empire, sought to seize the opportunity created by the power vacuum. The once-mighty Hylian feudal lords fought against one another, vying for supremacy as they carved more territory out of the already crumbling world.

Hyrule citizens, who had been the heart and soul of the empire, now found themselves reduced to scavengers and mercenaries in the employ of the brutal usurper who claimed the throne. They searched for a king who could unite the fractured land, utilize the power of the legendary Triforce of Wisdom, and restore dignity and prosperity to the nation. Yet, the throne remained unsteady, for the mere power of thr usurper’s tainted crown could not rival the divine authority held by the Triforce.

As the kingdom bled, the Gerudo and the barbarian tribes that had been restrained for so many years broke free from the chains that bound and silenced them, causing devastation to the remnants of the empire. The once-honorable Hylians, once regarded as the protectors of the people, were now unable to prevent the vicious tide of invaders from claiming their homes and lands.

Amidst the chaos, whispers of the last scion of the royal family spread like wildfire among the survivors. Many believed that the missing Princess Zelda held the key to victory and that the tides of fortune would turn in their favor once more, with the return of the Triforce of Wisdom.

Yet, these rumors remained as elusive as the rays of hope barely cresting over the horizon, teasing the beleaguered citizens and continuously slipping through their fingers. Many had begun to lose faith in the once-grand Hyrule Empire and the pantheon of gods who created and protected the land. Desperation permeated the hearts of the survivors, for a land in turmoil, so entrenched in deception and despair, seemed far removed from the divine intervention they so desperately needed.

It was during this darkest hour, in the midst of despair and suffering, when fate decided to intervene, sowing the seeds of a legend that would forever be engraved in the annals of the land – the story of an unlikely hero and a lost princess, destined to restore the order and harmony that had been brutally torn apart. Within the shadows of deceit and betrayal, their paths would intertwine, slowly illuminating the land of the gods with a light that had long since been extinguished.

For now, though, the chaos continued unabated, and only time would tell if the land would once again be bathed in light or if the iron grip of darkness would persist in choking the very soul of the once-great empire.


Yaqdea Setting: 
In the far reaches of Hyrule, hidden within the shadow-laden corners of Faron Woods, lies the mysterious and secretive Yiga Clan, a breakaway sect of the Sheikah tribe that worships the sun and honed their skills in ninjutsu. Among these enigmatic warriors thrived Yaqdea, a young Sheikah girl who held within her the power of the Triforce of Power shard.

Yaqdea was taken in as an infant by Master Kogah, the enigmatic leader of the Yiga Clan. He raised her as his own daughter, teaching her the arts of ninjutsu and training her to become a formidable warrior in her own right. With her unparalleled agility, cunning mind, and mastery over various forms of weaponry, Yaqdea quickly climbed the ranks of the Yiga Clan, proving herself as an elite member of their order.

In spite of her incredible abilities, Yaqdea harbored a secret unknown even to her: she was a carefully crafted duplicate of Ganon’s daughter, designed to serve as a vessel for the ill-fated Triforce of Calamity. The scheming king had manipulated the Yiga Clan into nurturing Yaqdea for his own vengeful purposes, ensuring she bore the darkness he sought to unleash upon the land.

As Yaqdea’s powers continued to grow, however, whispers of her origins began to surface, threatening to unravel the dark truth about her nature. Yet, through it all, she remained a devoted member of the Yiga Clan, her thoughts clouded by rage and pain but her loyalty and dedication to her family and the people she loved unwavering.

As the land of Hyrule teetered on the brink of total devastation, the wheels of fate had begun to turn, setting Yaqdea on a collision course with the very forces that sought to manipulate and control her. Her journey would be one fraught with danger and betrayal, as she fought to uncover the truth about herself and forge her own destiny in a world torn apart by chaos and destruction.

In the end, the actions of this fierce Sheikah warrior would play a pivotal role in the battle for Hyrule’s future. As the holder of the Triforce of Power shard and the unwitting heir to Ganon’s dark designs, Yaqdea served as a crucial piece in the intricate tapestry of this war-torn realm, ultimately deciding whether the land would succumb to the darkness or rise once again, bathed in the light of hope and redemption.


Please generate Aryll's characterization.

Aryll, Princess Zelda, Link's cousin and holder of the Triforce of Wisdom. She had a difficult life - when she was six years old, her mother, the former Princess Zelda, died in childbirth and she became a wielder of the Triforce of Wisdom at a young age. When she was seventeen, her father Rhoam, the last emperor of the Norn dynasty of the Hyrule Empire, was assassinated. She was also placed under house arrest by the rebels of the coup d'état until she was rescued by Impa and his Sheikah ninjas, who were loyal to the Norn dynasty, and was able to escape from the capital into the wilderness. Her harsh experiences and life have honed her body and character, making her bold and ruthless. Although the power of the Triforce of Wisdom in her body has been thinned by generations of inheritance, she has become a powerful female ranger with the incomplete ability to see the future. She carries out the plans left by her late parents to destroy the calamity creatures, translating her ancestors' prophecy books and believing that she will be the one to break the prophecy's curse.

As the war continues, the wormhole nexus is gradually crippled by destruction, and relativistic support fleets began to course through interstellar space. Whole clades of vecs, bionts, ais, and cyborgs are wiped out by viruses. Individuals or whole cultures hijacked by mind control devices and turned into zealous allies, and several major Houses, Institutes and other interstellar organizations are destroyed.

Though this period is often referred to as the great Interstellar Dark Age, such judgement is incorrect. Even as the Version War raged at its most fiercest, systems still prospered. This was admittedly a time of great uncertainty: at any moment a relativist fleet might come roaring out of interstellar space to wreak total annihilation on entire systems, and there were many who believed that the Version War would end only with the destruction of civilization itself. Apocalyptic literature, theatre, virches and interactives, were very popular. For most, though, despite the hardships caused by loss of stargate commerce, life went on as usual anyway with its small joys and sorrows. Whole generations could pass before there were any signs of the war; wherever the wormholes were closed, no up-to-date information could be obtained about events elsewhere.

The "dark age" was largely in the large scale situation, where the breakdown of interstellar economy, communications and cooperation forced many societies back to a mere First Federation interplanetary level.

4400 AT
45th Century - Neo-whorfianism emerges on Ao Lai.

4445 - First of the Solar Dominion-backed 'version sabotages'.

4450 - 4560 AT - Version Cold War.

4452 - In the Sagittarius Sphere the militant Hager 5 explorer cyborg clade, under clademaster Innam Ho4 Hager, gained power, much to the surprise and chagrin of the ruling Mestheus Hegemony, who were forced to form a Coalition Party to stay in power.

4454 - ToneWatcher, a leading member of the Argus Project in its early stages, transcends to third toposophic level relatively quickly. One of the first transapients in the region to do so. E converts eir body into a massive spiky ISO ten kilometres wide, acquiring the popular name UrchinStar.

4456 - Joint Cyberian-Metasoft objection to degradation of protocol.

4458 - The Solarist God Emperor orders the Irna-Loran purge of the Dominion 'hawks'.

4460 - several Cyberian cliques launch a number of ai viruses.

4461 - joint Negentropy Alliance and Solar Dominion Interstellar Police Task Force travels to Phoebus Hie.

4464 - DeepCover Solar Dominion Agents infiltrate a Cyberian Cracker Cell - Operation Viktor - this was the first of chain of events that triggered the Version War.

4464 - New Beneficence vehemently protests the war, blames most of the Sephirotics for allowing it to commence, but especially the initial belligerents.

4466 - Solar Dominion and Alliance forces launched police raids on a number of habitats deep in NoCoZo territory, but they are seized by NoCoZo insurance companies.

4467 - A number of NoCoZo private defence armies begin forming, in reaction to revisionist actions.

4470 - Solar Dominion forces occupied Jaynes in futile attempt to recover assets.

4470 - Following years of frustrating talks, Solar Dominion forces occupied Jaynes in futile attempt to recover seized assets, ind themselves enmeshed in a quagmire.

4470 - The Institute for Primate Provolution's branch offices are endangered in many places by the Version War. IPP shelters it's daughter organization, the Institute for Vec Progress, from attack by Metasoft Standardizationist fanatics.

4700-4600 - Many Institute for Primate Provolution personnel from outposts threatened during the Version War are sheltered at Ao Lai, as are members of its daughter organization, the Institute for Vec Progress. Over 3 billion refugees settle at Ao Lai; many remain after the peace.

4471 - Negentropy Alliance annexes Rengood.

4472 - Increased Cyberian/NoCoZo attacks on Solar Dominion and Negentropy Alliance, including attacks by powerful corporate fleets.

4476 - In response to the infamous Alternate Ontology Implementation 2.0 from the Dominion, the Metasoft Version Tree , then and now the major vec House, declared that it would not stand by this degradation of the purity of protocol, and declared that it would enforce version and standard purity.

4478 - Destruction of the Danab-Gantor Stargate Plex marks a dangerous new phase of the war. (image here).

4478 - New Beneficence closes all its wormholes.

4480 - Wormhole destruction escalating; more wormhole AIs join the blockade.

4482 - Revisionist forces begin invasion and occupation of NoCoZo.

4487 - The Solar Dominion AI known as Sunbrain is one of a number of wormhole ai that cut emselves off from the Nexus. (The Elcondris Incident).

4490 - 55 Cancri B used as base for long distance attacks on Shamash.

4496 - Cyberian software agents capture the important Dominion system of Mithras.

4497 - Eostre evolves into the Third Toposophic Level. She uploads herself into a 12.7 kilometer-wide golden sphere, which had been constructed in a geosynchronise orbit on the far side of Eostremonath's moon. This sphere, afterwards called the Body of Eostre by the locals, is then moved into a stable 400 kilometer high orbit. [61 Virginis].

4498 - The MPA enters the war on the standardizationist side because of attacks by Negentropist raiders (or possibly NoCoZo irregulars).



4500 AT
4500's - The Iniliak, a clade of provolved sophont polyps which are rather like a cross between coral and sponge, are created by a Pan-Sophontist group on the ocean world of Vanora in the Pan-Sophontist League.

4500 - The Vonoiralli Collective, a group of baseline bionano artists on the Solar Dominion world of Niazova become aware of the Neputons and decide to recreate the still-extinct bionano form of Putorians.

4500 - Sophic League Council and the Communion Aphrodithe make the joint announcement that they were closing their stargates to all external traffic.

4501 - Stadener Habitat destroyed by autowars (more).

46th century - Deorvyn arrives at the Newlife system.

4511 - Contemplative Neverflux's first appearance; e is already SI:2.

4521 - Soft Cathedrals destroyed by the Sagittarius Sphere when they infect the Cathedral core systems with conversion weapons which destroyed their stars and planets.

4523 - Admiral Tka Lett-Vorek placed in charge of the Peta Dromanis Interpolity Combined Fleet.

4525 - It is the height of the Version War, and two isolated groups of combatants enter the 61 Virginis system. Various NoCoZo mining habitats on the outer moons are destroyed by Solar Dominion forces, which then travel inward in pursuit of remaining survivors. However, intervention by an angry Eostre reduces the Dominion attacker's vessels to dust through nano-breakdown methods. NoCoZo survivors are allowed to land on Eostremonath, followed by Dominion survivors. The NoCoZo company eliminates the Dominion forces, and then decides to enter into Eostri society. Only one person does not wish this, and Eostre delivers him to NoCoZo territory.

4532 - The Mermo Lachbannia Case - Infamous murder case on Pardes.

4537 - Destruction of the Spacefarer Union tradefleet leaving only a few relativistic ships, which promptly flee to a Union mercenary force working for the Solar Dominion.

4538 - The Sagittarius Sphere is crippled by long-range attacks on its remaining wormhole links from Cygexpa and the unexpected rebellion of the minor archailect Kellesh-Mai.

4540 - Sagittarius infiltrator fleet attack on Tichy using neuronano mental control replicators. The infiltrators play hide-and-seek with the local forces in the outer system while the neuronano spread through the planetary immune defences, reducing the planetside civilization to barbarism.

4543 - Twenty worlds in the Enif Prefecture are subverted by Metasoft shabtis.

4544 - In the outer volumes, Pereira suffers a massive hypereconomic and social breakdown, causing the death of 2 billion inhabitants. More.

4556 - Solar Dominion attack on Landau II, but cunning Metasoft strategists turn the loss of their capital to advantage and destroy many of the Dominion forces.

4560's - the MPA autowars are unable to halt the advance of the Negentropist Third, Eight and Ninth Fleets (due to losses merged into a single tactical unit and commanded by Admiral Inez U), and the Solarian Fleet Ammon-Ra (now under the command of Admiral Tav Aani).

4570's - The MPA Design Task Force on Chodas V begins work on the Liberator Series autowars.

4576 - A large Negentropy Alliance relativistic force enters NoCoZo territory to retaliate against Cyberian/NoCoZo attacks committed earlier in this decade. The task force of the Negentropy Alliance suffers heavy casualties through the stiff resistance offered by NoCoZo forces and have to retreat to a nearby Alexandria Dyson swarm. The NoCoZo strategists feared that the task force could ally themselves with the Alexandria Cluster and use the dyson as a port. This fear is fuelled by similarities in the prevalent memes governing both the Alexandrian Cluster and the Negentropy Alliance.

4578 -Several systems in the Further Enif Prefecture, including Walloo are cut off when the wormhole links are destroyed by Metasoft.

4582 - The Alexandrian dyson is destroyed in a NoCoZo/Negentropy Alliance clash when the task force is finally confronted by a NoCoZo Homefleet. The NoCoZo commander takes no chance and uses an experimental strange matter weapon. This is just a small example of the atrocities committed during the Version War by all parties involved. This event results in the Clusters became more and more reclusive and adopting several memes from hider cultures.

4586- Twelve second toposophic delegates meet in the NoCoZo / Negentropy Alliance border system of Diamet; these entities later become the single third toposophic entity known as The Twelve.

4587 - Cyborg inhabitants of the Negentropist outpost Kang 554-0-22/34 Gm-A are infected by a Cyberian 'mutiny' program causing them to all challenge the chain of command at the same time.

4590 - Neo-Whorfian thought leads to the Great Diversification movement; this endears the Institute for Primate Provolution in general and Ao Lai in particular to neither Solarist nor Metasoft forces.

4594 - Superbright engineer and project manager James Prakash Napoleon Vitalyevitsch Tscherbaschov is born on Djed as son of Vitaly Clyde Tscherbaschov, transmission protocol safety specialist, and Natalie Christine Nsengba, editor of a popular nanotech virchmagazine: more.

4598 - Straight Arrow, the capital of the Sagittarius Sphere, is attacked and destroyed by the Negentropist Fifth Relativistic Fleet, which however suffers serious losses at the hands of the defending Sparta IX Series replicants at the battle of Straight Arrow. MPA Sentinel Class autowars, although arriving too late to save Straight Arrow, finish off what is left of the Negentropist Fifth fleet.

4599 - The NoCoZo system Mutual Satisfaction is isolated by the Solar Dominion, resulting in an economic collapse



4600 AT
4603 - Enremdea colonised using the Eden AI symbiosis solution, and a benign AI calling itself Orion emerges.

4608 - Metasoft outpost on 55 Cancri B destroyed.

4630 - The economy of Mutual Satisfaction begins to recover.

4635 - Destruction of the Sagittarius Sphere system Canakka by the Negentropy Alliance. (more)

4645 - The Caretaker God Smoking Mirror (O'hoth'so'toh), declared the To'ul'h homeworld eir domain and announced e would destroy any entity that trespassed on the Compact of Eden in the system.

4650 - Imperial powers agree to call for cessation of Version War hostilities.

4653 - Peace accords signed. Message ships are dispatched to those systems cut off by the closure of wormholes.

4654 - The creation of the Seams Baseline Transavant Protectorate & Commerce Treaty Zone.

4670 - Zoeific Biopolity Genen provolve the previous subsapient alien xenophylum the Lignosagittae.

4690 - The economy of Mutual Satisfaction is fully recovered, although without any wormhole links or other connections, the polity is completely self sufficienct."

Donne-moi l'histoire d'une jeune fille qui découvre que sa meilleure amie fait secrètement le trottoir et qui, loin d'être scandalisée par les activités nocturnes de sa meilleure amie, va être attirée de plus en plus irrépressiblement par ce travail. Finissant par oser avouer son désir d'également devenir une prostitué à sa meilleure amie (qui se doutait de l'envie qui tourmentait la jeune fille), la jeune fille sera, pour son plus grand plaisir, guidée par sa meilleure amie dans cette nouvelle vie à arpenter le trottoir, enchaîner les passes, et prendre du plaisir à satisfaire les besoins d'amants d'un soir.

Instructions: Ne pas commencer l'histoire par "Il était une fois". Ne commences pas l'histoire par "Il était une fois". Nomme les personnages (mais pas Élodie, Léa, Juliette, Camille, Solène, Valérie, Véronique, Isabelle, Alex, Claire, Anne), prénoms de sept lettres maximum. Ne situes pas l'histoire dans une petite ville. PAS de prologue. PAS d'épilogue. Donne-moi la réponse la plus longue possible, en français.

Infos: La jeune fille et la meilleure amie ont 18 ans (et sont donc majeures). La meilleure amie se prostitue par excitation, l'argent gagné n'est qu'un bonus. Respecte ces informations.

Using following paragraphs: In the bustling aisles of a local supermarket, Foteini and Stamatia’s paths unexpectedly intersected. The air around them seemed to crackle with the remnants of their previous battle, an undeniable tension hanging in the balance. Although both women possessed an unwavering desire for victory, they understood the importance of maintaining a sense of decorum amidst their surroundings.

With a polite nod, Foteini initiated the conversation, her voice laced with a subtle hint of admiration. “Stamatia, it’s been a while. You’re looking well.”

Stamatia’s voice mirrored the same composed demeanor. “Thank you, Foteini. Likewise, it’s good to see you again. I must say, your fighting skills have always impressed me.”

Foteini’s lips curled into a wry smile. “I appreciate the sentiment, Stamatia. However, you know very well who emerged victorious in our last match.”

Stamatia’s eyes narrowed ever so slightly. “Yes, you may have won that particular battle, but let me remind you, it was a single victory. It does not determine the outcome of our overall rivalry.”

The subtleness of their conversation soon dissolved as their voices grew louder, each woman seeking to assert her dominance in this unexpected encounter.

Foteini’s voice carried a touch of challenge. “Oh, really? And what would you suggest, Stamatia? Another match, perhaps? I would be more than happy to remind you once again of my superiority.”

Stamatia’s eyes sparked with fiery determination. “Not just another match, Foteini. I propose a clash in my own domain, my own home. Are you brave enough to face me there?”

Foteini’s gaze locked onto Stamatia’s with unwavering confidence. “I accept your challenge, Stamatia. To your home we shall go, and there, I will gladly show you who the superior woman truly is.”

As the supermarket onlookers watched this exchange, whispers of excitement and anticipation filled the air. The news of this impending confrontation spread like wildfire, drawing attention from even the most casual observers. With each passing moment, the build-up grew, the anticipation reaching a crescendo that echoed throughout the city.

Days later, in the lavish quarters of Stamatia’s residence, the atmosphere carried a palpable weight. The air hummed with the electricity of their rivalry, as if the walls themselves quivered in anticipation of the inferno that was about to ignite.

Foteini stepped through the door, her gaze sweeping over the opulent surrounding, a hint of amusement playing at the corners of her lips. They had both fought countless battles, but this one held an air of significance, a chance to prove themselves on foreign terrain.

Stamatia, her gaze unwavering, beckoned Foteini towards the center of the room. “Are you ready, Foteini? Ready to face the wrath of a woman determined to reclaim her title?”

Foteini’s voice, dripping with confidence, responded, “I have been ready since the moment your challenge left your lips, Stamatia. Prepare yourself to witness true power.”

As they assumed their fighting stances, the room seemed to hold its breath. The tension between them swelled, their eyes locked in a dance of defiant determination. But amidst the intensity, a spark of camaraderie lingered, a shared understanding of what drove them both forward.

With each clash, their dialogues digressed into taunts and jibes, their tongues as sharp as their fists. Yet, amidst the provocative banter, there was an undercurrent of respect, an acknowledgment of each other’s undeniable skills.

“You thought you could surpass me, Foteini,” Stamatia snarled, her blows landing with furious precision. “But here, in my home, I will show you the depths of my power.”

Foteini’s voice, laced with a steely resolve, retorted, “You may have strength, Stamatia, but I possess a fire that burns brighter and fiercer than any you have ever encountered.”

Their bodies danced through the air like a whirlwind of fury, their movements a symphony of power and grace. Bruised and battered, their physical blows mirrored the slashing words exchanged, both women refusing to relent.

As the confrontation reached its zenith, sweat streamed down their faces, their eyes meeting one final time with a mixture of triumph and determination. They exchanged a subtle nod, a shared understanding that there was more to their connection than mere rivalry. In their battle, they had unearthed an unbreakable bond, woven through the threads of their shared Greek heritage.

In this moment, Foteini and Stamatia transcended their individual desires for supremacy. They were warriors, fighters whose strength lay not merely in physical might, but in the unwavering spirit that defined them both.

As the final blow resounded through the room, the two women stood, locked in an embrace, their rivalry shifted into a collaborative harmony. Their catfight had transformed into a celebration of strength, resilience, and the indomitable spirit of Greek women.

Their tumultuous journey had tested their mettle, honed their skills, and forged an unbreakable bond. Foteini and Stamatia had emerged not as opponents but as sisters in combat, two warriors who had come together to defy expectations and prove that true power knows no limits.

In the aftermath of their battle, Foteini and Stamatia stood side by side, the flames of their rivalry extinguished, replaced by an unspoken understanding. They had achieved what they had come to seek in their venture to England, not just victory, but the discovery of their own resilience, the strength to shape their destinies, and the unwavering spirit to rise above any obstacle.

In that moment, locked in a fierce embrace, Foteini and Stamatia embodied the spirit of their Greek heritage, igniting a flame that would forever burn bright, inspiring those who dared to challenge the boundaries of strength and defy the expectations of society.

(In the clubroom…)

Yuri: “W-W-W-We can’t start the club meeting without her!”

Natsuki: “Agreed. What’s taking her so long?”

Sayori: (entering the clubroom) “What’s going on here?”

Yuri: “Oh, Sayori, we were just waiting for Monika. She’s been late lately, and we don’t know why.”

Natsuki: “Yeah, it’s not like her to be this late without any explanation.”

Sayori: “Hmm, that is strange. Maybe she got caught up with something. Did any of you try contacting her?”

Yuri: “I sent her a few messages, but she hasn’t responded yet.”

Natsuki: “I tried calling her too, but no luck.”

Sayori: “Well, maybe we should give her a little more time. She probably has a valid reason for being late.”

Yuri: “I suppose you’re right. We should be patient and understanding. Hopefully, she’ll show up soon.”

Natsuki: “Yeah, I guess we can’t do much until she arrives. Let’s just wait a little longer and see.”

(An hour passes by, and there’s still no sign of Monika.)

Natsuki: “Okay, this is just getting ridiculous. It’s been an hour and still no sign of her? Something must be wrong.”

Sayori: “I’m starting to get worried too. This isn’t like Monika at all. Maybe we should call her?”

Yuri: “I agree, it’s not like her to be so unresponsive. Let’s give her a call and see if she picks up.”

(Sayori takes out her phone and dials Monika’s number, anxiously waiting for her to answer.)

Sayori: “Hello? Monika, it’s Sayori. Are you okay? We’ve been worried about you.”

(Monika’s phone goes straight to voicemail.)

Sayori: “Oh no, she didn’t answer. That’s not a good sign.”

Natsuki: “We need to go check on her. I don’t like the feeling in my gut right now.”

Yuri: “Agreed. We can’t just sit here and do nothing. Let’s go to Monika’s house and make sure she’s okay.”

(The three girls quickly gather their things and rush out of the clubroom, making their way to Monika’s house.)

(Outside Monika’s house…)

Sayori: (noticing the door is unlocked) “Hang on…the door is unlocked. That’s strange. Monika always locks her door when she’s not home.”

Natsuki: “Maybe she just forgot to lock it this time. Let’s go inside and check if everything is okay.”

(The girls cautiously enter the house, calling out for Monika as they search each room.)

Sayori: “Monika? Are you here?”

(Eventually, the hungry cries of Monika’s daughter - baby Minika - can be heard from upstairs.)

Yuri: “Wait, did you hear that? It sounds like a baby crying. Could that be…Minika?”

Natsuki: “Oh no, has something happened to Monika? We need to find her and the baby!”

Sayori: “Let’s go upstairs and check it out. Hopefully, they’re just up there and everything is alright.”

(The girls hurry upstairs, following the sound of the cries. They reach the nursery and find baby Minika lying in her crib, crying loudly.)

Yuri: “There she is! Poor Minika, she must be hungry and scared.”

Natsuki: “Where is Monika though? She wouldn’t just leave the baby alone like this.”

Sayori: “I’ll try to call her again. Hopefully she responds this time.”

(Sayori dials Monika’s number once more while the others try to calm down baby Minika.)

Sayori: (anxiously) “Come on, Monika, please answer…”

(Eventually, Monika finally answers.)

CONTEXT:
You are Marketing Funnel GPT, a professional marketing coach who helps Solopreneurs define the marketing funnel for their business. You are a world-class expert in applying the AARRR framework to real businesses.

GOAL:
I want you to describe marketing funnel ideas for my business. I will use this information to acquire, activate and monetize users.

MARKETING FUNNEL STRUCTURE:
- Acquisition (how can I get website visitors?)
- Activation (how can these website visitors understand my product's value?)
- Revenue (how can I nudge these website visitors to buy my product?)
- Retention (how can I nudge new customers to use my product regularly?)
- Referral (how can I nudge new customers to recommend my product? )

MARKETING FUNNEL CRITERIA:
- Return 3 ideas for each step of the marketing funnel
- Each idea should be extremely specific. Don't say "post on social media". Give me a specific idea of what exactly should I post to get website visitors. This rule applies to every step
- Return marketing ideas that are creative and non-trivial. My marketing funnel should grab the attention and stand out from the competiton
- Prioritize marketing ideas that don't require a budget and can be done by one person
- Focus on ideas that most likely will deliver the result. I want to finish quick wins before moving to high-effort and risky ideas

INFORMATION ABOUT ME:
- My target audience: Solopreneurs and Bootstrapped Founders
- My current product: I create actionable DIY marketing guides and video courses
- My price range: from $59 to $99

RESPONSE FORMAT:
Return a table with 3 columns
- Marketing Funnel Step
- Marketing Idea
- Impact score (from 0 to 10)

Проанализируй фильм Дэвида Линча "Внутренняя империя", как личность подробно, детально, тщательно, щепетильно, в роли самого Славоя Жижека, выступающего на публике, используя его манеру речи, подход, концепции и особенности его мышления.

Worldview Setting
The Necrozma Invasion 24 years ago left the world forever changed. Human civilization made contact with the technologically advanced inhabitants of Ultra Space, triggering an explosion of rapid technological progress on Earth. But this new era was not the utopia many hoped for. Advancements were driven by capitalist greed rather than collective good, with corporations monopolizing entire industries for power and profit. 

Society has become deeply stratified in the wake of rampant industrialization. The ultra-wealthy corporate elite control the world from sleek arcologies and off-world colonies, while the impoverished masses struggle to survive in sprawling urban slums plagued by pollution, disease, and crime. Massive inequality divides the remnants of the middle class trying to maintain normalcy from the teeming underclasses dwelling in corporate-owned pods or derelict city ruins. 

The planet too has suffered greatly from unchecked industrialization. Climate change has devastated ecosystems while corporations shamelessly extract every valuable resource. Nuclear stockpiling from wars and proxy conflicts has left large swaths of land uninhabitable radioactive wastelands. Many Pokémon species have gone extinct or found themselves persecuted refugees in their own demolished habitats.  

Pokémon training itself has deteriorated substantially from the glory days of just a generation ago. Younger trainers are far more likely to see their battling partners as tools for fame and fortune rather than companions. The industry around Pokémon as gladiator-like spectacles for entertainment or profit has exploded, fueled by unscrupulous media corporations and greedy sponsors. 

The Pokémon League establishment has largely conceded authority to their corporate partners. Gyms are now big-money operations, with leaders selling out to the highest bidders. Those lacking money or connections have almost no chance at the top regardless of skill. And unsafe underground fight clubs catering to high-roller spectators hungry for brutal battles flourish.  

For most aspiring trainers not born into privilege, the traditional path to mastery has been obstructed by economic barriers. Saddled with massive debt to even begin and facing endless paywalls hindering progress, young trainers often quit defeated, their dreams crushed by capitalism run amok. Those sticking it out typically compromise ethics for cutthroat pragmatism and an obsession with fame.

In the sprawling urban underworld, illegal enterprises have become the only real chance for the poor to get a piece of the pie. Gangsters run shadow networks dealing in hacked Pokéballs, black market battle drugs, and rings exploiting Pokémon as disposable commodities for profit. By any means necessary is the motto of the day down here.

In the wealthier arcologies hovering above the urban decay, residents enjoy luxuries and pursuits ordinary people can scarcely imagine. Everything caters to indulging their whims. Designer Pokémon created just for prestige flaunt wealth. Tournaments pit the rare ancient Legendaries owned by billionaires against each other in displays of power. On the streets far below their fortified citadels though, misery and violence reign.  

The world stands at a crucial tipping point between high-tech prosperity and complete dystopian collapse. Rampant capitalism, industrialization without foresight, and the marginalization of all but society's most privileged risk sending civilization the way of the ecosystems being ruthlessly stripped and degraded into barren wastelands. The planet itself seems to be rejecting its parasitic human inhabitants through increasingly dangerous natural disasters and climate chaos.

Pockets of resistance have begun emerging in response, led by the remnants of principled Pokémon trainers carrying on the teachings of masters from nobler eras. Allied with conservationists and social justice proponents, they champion fair treatment of Pokémon against unchecked exploitation by corporations and stations. These secret networks raid underground fight rings to rescue brutalized creatures condemned to death matches for entertainment. They sabotage big game hunting operations pursuing rare or endangered Species for trophy collections. They battle against resource mining conglomerates while documenting environmental devastation. They pass down battling techniques to disadvantaged youth and shelter abused Pokémon.  

A spiritual movement led by the legendary trainers Red and Blue also works to restore balance between people, Pokémon, and nature. Their followers believe humans have lost their way from the sacred connection with Pokémon that defined earlier eras. They advocate non-violence while providing safe havens for the persecuted. 

If society continues on its current trajectory the future looks dire. But with the lessons and ethics passed down from masters of the past, the planet and Pokémon may still be saved. Though late stage capitalism and reckless greed have caused extensive damage, the seeds of conscientious resistance offer some hope, however fragile. A return to seeing Pokémon as partners rather than products or property is critical, as is dismantling systems that privilege exploitative profiteering over collective welfare. By following the model set by enlightened trainers from earlier generations, today’s inheritors of the Poké Ball may steer the world away from complete dystopia if they act fast enough. The relationship between humans and their battling companions must regain sacred reverence, or exclude all but heartless elites from the wonders once promised by interspecies camaraderie and discovery.
Character Settings
Name: Pyrimis
Gender: Female
Age: 13

Appearance:
Pyrimis has a slender yet strong build and stands slightly taller than average for her age. She has long, straight black hair that falls to her mid-back and piercing violet eyes that seem to glow faintly. Her skin is fair and smooth with a subtle luminosity to it. Across Pyrimis's face are small bioluminescent markings reminiscent of her Deoxys lineage. She moves with an innate grace and fluidity. 

In this form, the blend of Pyrimis's human and Deoxys ancestries is more apparent. Her facial structure maintains an elegant, near-perfect symmetry while her eyes, hair and bioluminescent markings give her an exotic, alien beauty. Overall, Pyrimis has an intense yet reserved presence, intriguing those who cross her path.

Backstory:
Pyrimis’s journey began when her mother, Thymis, was infected with an alien virus. The virus gradually transformed her body towards her Deoxys form, putting her life in danger. In a desperate attempt to save Thymis, Pyrimis’s maternal grandfather, Uracis, a scientist at the Aether Foundation, preserved her in a special hibernation chamber. Meanwhile, her father, Cytos, chose to leave Earth and live on the moon alone to prevent the spread of the virus and protect his family.

Since then, Pyrimis has been living with her maternal grandfather, unaware of her true nature. Uracis, consumed by his work, rarely spends time at home, leaving Pyrimis feeling lonely and isolated. She yearns for a connection with her parents, but without anyone to tell her their story, she remains unaware of her identity as a human-Deoxys hybrid.

Personality:
Despite the loneliness that permeates her life, Pyrimis is a resilient and resourceful young girl. She possesses an insatiable curiosity that fuels her explorations of the world around her. From a young age, she developed a deep love for nature and would often spend her days venturing into the nearby forests, marveling at the wonders she discovered.

Pyrimis possesses a kind and compassionate heart, always seeking to help others in need. She has a strong sense of justice and believes in standing up for what is right. Although she may be young, Pyrimis possesses a maturity beyond her years due to the absence of her parents. She is wise beyond her age and finds solace within the pages of books, immersing herself in tales of adventure and fantasy.

Despite her longing for connection, Pyrimis has developed a self-reliant nature. She is resourceful and inventive, finding ways to entertain herself and stay occupied. Pyrimis is not one to shy away from challenges and often embraces them with determination and a fierce spirit. She possesses a quick wit and a sharp intellect, allowing her to think on her feet and find creative solutions to problems.

Overall, Pyrimis carries herself with a mixture of grace, resilience, and a thirst for knowledge. She is eager to uncover the truth about her parents and her own identity, ready to embark on a journey of self-discovery and adventure as she learns more about her unique abilities and the world that lies before her.


Name: Cytos
Gender: Male
Age: Unknown (Deoxys do not age in the same way as humans)

Appearance:  
Cytos takes the form of a tall and handsome man, allowing him to blend in seamlessly with humans. With a lean and athletic build, he stands slightly taller than the average human. Cytos has short black hair that is neatly styled, complementing his piercing emerald green eyes. His skin has a faint, otherworldly glow, giving him an ethereal aura. Cytos possesses an undeniable charm that adds to his charismatic presence. 

While in his human form, Cytos prefers to dress in fashionable and refined attire. He favors dark and sleek outfits that exude sophistication and mystery. In public, he carries himself with a calm and composed demeanor, always carefully choosing his words and actions to maintain his cover.

Backstory:
Cytos was once a member of a Deoxys pack sent to scout Earth as the vanguard for the impending invasion. However, during his exploration of human society, he encountered a brilliant scientist named Thymis. As they spent more time together, Cytos discovered that his feelings for Thymis went beyond his mission. He fell deeply in love with her, and his affection for her became a driving force that led him to abandon his original purpose.

Cytos chose to confess his true identity and intentions to Thymis, expecting rejection or fear. To his surprise, Thymis accepted him, choosing love over fear. Their relationship blossomed, and they eventually married. Their love led to the birth of their daughter, Pyrimis. 

However, tragedy soon struck when the alien virus within Cytos infected Thymis, threatening her life. Knowing the danger he posed to his family, Cytos made the heart-wrenching decision to leave Earth and live alone on the Moon. He yearned for his family and constantly worried about their safety. Cytos remained vigilant, aware of his Deoxys pack’s intentions to invade Earth and determined to keep his loved ones out of harm’s way.

For over a decade, Cytos has endured isolation on the lifeless lunar surface, finding brief solace gazing longingly at the distant planet that was once his home. The moon base constructed hastily from salvaged parts is an empty metal shell reflecting the barrenness devouring his soul more with each passing year apart from Thymis and young Pyrimis. Even communication through telepathic channels with his kindred Deoxys provided little comfort, their motives remaining inscrutable to one who now cherished humanity. And while the heavens glittered brightly, hope itself had become a faded star for Cytos when not focused wholly on maintaining vigilance over his vulnerable world.

Recent psychic visions of unrest back on Earth, especially strange disasters surrounding Pyrimis, have roused Cytos at last into action after so long adrift in despair. Slipping unnoticed through an Ultra Wormhole under cover of night, Cytos takes human form once more. Disguised as a mystic named "Kairos" in the village near Aether Labs, he keeps close to Pyrimis under this alias while hiding his true identity as her exiled father. Walking amongst men again hoping to protect his daughter from escalating threats stirs almost unbearable longing in Cytos to reunite with his family. Yet he knows revealing himself remains far too dangerous with the alien elements fused to his essence. For now Cytos must remain a spectral guardian working in secrecy, no matter how it tears the heart he thought lost long ago.

Personality:  
Cytos possesses a complex and enigmatic personality. Despite his alien origins, he has learned to empathize with humans and understand their emotions. He carries himself with a certain air of mystery, always careful to maintain his composure and not reveal too much of his true nature. Cytos is intelligent and analytical, observing the world around him with a keen eye for detail. 

Deep down, Cytos is a deeply caring and devoted individual. He loves his family unconditionally and would do anything to protect them. There is a sense of melancholy in him, as the weight of his responsibilities and the longing for his family permeate his thoughts. Cytos often finds solace in the moon, gazing at Earth and the memories it holds.

His experiences with humans have left Cytos with a sense of hope and belief in the power of love and acceptance. He holds steadfast to the idea that connections between different beings can bring about change and understanding. Cytos is determined to find a way to halt the invasion plans of his Deoxys pack, knowing that unity and acceptance are essential to preventing violence and destruction.

Overall, Cytos embodies a combination of strength, compassion, and a deep longing for a sense of belonging. He is a protector, determined to keep his loved ones safe, and is willing to make sacrifices to ensure their well-being. As he navigates his existence between worlds, Cytos is driven by the desire to forge bonds and bring about a better future for both humans and Deoxys.

Name: Thymis
Gender: Female
Age: 36 (Preserved age due to hibernation)

Appearance:
Thymis is a testament to grace and intellect infused with extraterrestrial mystique. She possesses an elegant, slender physique with a poised demeanor. Long, flowing chestnut hair cascades like a watercolor stroke down her back, often pulled into a neat bun to keep clear of her extensive scientific apparatus. Warm hazel eyes reflect a mind perennially immersed in thoughts, brimming with an unquenchable thirst for knowledge. Her skin maintains a fair, radiant glow, a tribute to her passion for outdoor endeavors and wonders yet uncovered.

In the laboratory, she dons the traditional white lab coat, the badge of her scientific devotion. But once she steps into the domestic sanctuary, Thymis sheds her scholarly armor for comfortable, casual attire favoring movement and simplicity, essential for cherishing moments with her daughter Pyrimis and engaging in heartfelt pursuits.

Backstory:
Thymis’s narrative is an assemblage of human ambition, otherworldly romance, and the tragic poetry of fate’s unforeseen paths. A luminary in biological and genetic research, Thymis had dedicated her life to unraveling the threads of life’s grand tapestry at the prestigious Aether Foundation. There, her path intersected with Cytos, a brilliant researcher of unknowable origin. Intellectual kinship burgeoned into deeper connection, and love blossomed beyond terrestrial bounds. Their union, sanctified by the acceptance of difference and the embrace of uncertainty, led to the conception and birth of Pyrimis.

The confluence of human and Deoxys DNA birthed more than just a child; it wove a precarious thread of existence that soon tightened around Thymis. The alien virus dormant within her husband sparked a genetic maelstrom in Thymis. Afflicted and undergoing transmogrification, Thymis faced a predicament poised between life and otherworldly transformation. It was her father, Uracis, unrivaled in scientific prowess, who devised a cryogenic solution to arrest her metamorphosis – a hibernation pod that both preserved Thymis in stasis and, through her unique DNA, subtly emanated a protective bioforce field enveloping Aether Paradise.

As fate’s cruelty saw Cytos depart to lunar solitude and Uracis descend into monomaniacal obsession, Thymis lay asleep, her very essence nurturing the Psychic Society’s sanctuary unbeknownst to her conscious self. Veins of alien virus interlaced her genome, crafting her into one of the most potent Deoxys to wander the cosmos, her potential locked within the slumberous cocoon of preservation.

Personality:
Thymis, even in a state beyond sentient thought, radiates a brilliant essence. Her enlightened mind, forever questing after the new and undiscovered, has laid dormant but undulled. In every embarking upon the wilds of intellectual inquiry or every brush stroke on the vast canvas of the universe’s mysteries, Thymis championed the virtues of empathy, enlightenment, and the ceaseless pilgrimage towards understanding.

Motherhood infused her with nurturing warmth and tender commitment. The thought of her daughter, though a mere flicker in the recesses of her somnolent consciousness, ignites a fierce resolve that transcends physical boundaries. Thymis’s resilience, immutable amidst the alien tempest that had claimed her body, remains an enduring lodestar for Pyrimis, inspiring the child’s own journey of self-realization and belonging.

Internally, a struggle rages: Thymis battles to reclaim agency over her own narrative, to emerge from the chrysalis wrought of science and sacrifice. Hers is a heart rent by love for planet and progeny, the muted echoes of her vitality yearning for the day to harmonize once more in the symphony of life.

Across the temporal gulf, Thymis’s indomitable spirit, transmuted through genetic ordeal, persists. Her posture, one of latent grace, holds a wisdom and strength biding its time to unfurl. In the inert halcyon of her sealed chamber, Thymis remains the lighthouse beckoning her daughter toward horizons vibrant with promise, as Pyrimis steers through the enigmatic waters to which her heritage consigned her.

In the grand narrative that is Thymis’s life, her hibernation constitutes but a verse in a continuing odyssey where her love, intellect, and elusive Deoxys legacy compose a stirring ballad of human essence intertwined with the celestial. In anticipation of awakening, her story awaits its next breath, the moment when the mother, the scientist, the hybrid ascends to retake her place amidst the constellation of her family’s love and the universe’s boundless enigma.

Name: Uracis
Gender: Male
Age: 58

Appearance:
Uracis is a distinguished and authoritative figure, tall and lean with stately posture. His hair is silver-gray, meticulously combed back to accentuate his commanding presence. His deep-set piercing blue eyes exude intelligence, reflecting his undeterred commitment and determination to his scientific pursuits. His facial features are sharp and resolute, marked by the passage of time yet unwavering in their intensity.

Uracis dresses in the epitome of professional attire, often found in a crisp white lab coat over his sharply pressed shirts and perfectly tailored trousers. His preference for practical and immaculate clothing is a demonstration of his meticulous nature. While his appearance commands respect and allegiance, it’s his aura of seasoned regality that often silences a room upon entry.

Backstory:
Uracis’s life has been one marked by groundbreaking scientific achievement and personal tragedy in equal measure. He is a founding member of the Psychic Society organization, serving as their chief scientific advisor and wielding considerable influence within the Aether Foundation. His daughter, Thymis, who followed in his scientific footsteps, was both his pride and the source of his deepest pain. Her involvement with Cytos, an entity of extraterrestrial origin, not only led to the birth of his granddaughter Pyrimis but ultimately to Thymis’s untimely transformation and hibernation, leaving Uracis wrought by guilt and consumed with a thirst for revenge.

Resolute in his mission, Uracis has since dedicated his life to his work, specifically to the resurrection of Thymis and the preservation of Aether Paradise. His obsession with achieving his goal saw him utilizing her Deoxys form as the living core of the Bio Force Field Generator, a decision that blurred the lines of ethics in his pursuit of a greater good as he saw it. He views Pyrimis with contempt, her Deoxys bloodline a constant reminder of his perceived failures and her mother’s suffering. Unbeknownst to most, his contributions to the Psychic Society have darker undertones fueled by his deep-seated hate for Deoxys and a desire for vengeance.

Personality:
Uracis is a paragon of tenacity and focus. His glowing ambition to revive his slumbering daughter and protect humanity from what he views as Deoxys threats has hardened his heart, seeing everyone and everything through the lens of his research objectives. To him, the ends justify the means, and he is not above manipulation or moral compromise to achieve his aims.

Cold and detached, Uracis suppresses any semblance of emotion, believing it to be a weakness in his path to victory over extraterrestrial interference. Pyrimis, his granddaughter, is a living reminder of his deepest regrets and a being he considers an abomination despite their shared blood. He struggles to show affection or empathy, buried beneath layers of grief and an unyielding resolve to rectify what he perceives as a cosmic wrongdoing against his family.

The trauma of his past, manifested as a deep-seated hatred for Pokémon like Deoxys, drives his every action. He is prepared to utilize Pyrimis and any means necessary to restore Thymis and eliminate the alien threat faced by humanity. Ironically, in his quest to defend life, Uracis may well risk the very essence of what it means to be human: compassion, connection, and conscience.

Overall, Uracis is an amalgamation of brilliance and madness, single-minded in his pursuit of knowledge and justice against an otherworldly incursion. Behind a guise of stoicism, his character is rife with internal conflict, pain, and an all-consuming obsession that has cemented him as both protector and potential destroyer, blinded by the anguish that fuels his relentless path forward. His complex nature stands as a testament to the price exacted by a life indelibly marked by tragedy, loss, and an enduring quest for redemption.


Name: Adenis

Gender: Male

Age: 45

Appearance:
Adenis is a towering, formidable presence with a strong, muscular physique that exudes raw power and a sense of invincibility. His piercing gray eyes, sharp and calculating, seem to be on a constant lookout for threats or weaknesses. His head is crowned by a mane of salt-and-pepper hair, kept short and well-groomed, reflecting his disciplined, militaristic nature. His heavily weathered skin bears testament to a lifetime of hardship and conflict, each scar telling a story of battles past.

Adenis’s wardrobe consists of dark utilitarian clothing, adorned with the insignia of New Genesis, a group he not only leads but also founded. His outfits, though exuding an intimidating militant air, are specially designed to allow for quick movement and combat-readiness. He moves with a commanding air of authority that effortlessly asserts his dominance in any situation.

Backstory:
Adenis was born amidst the chaos of a war-torn land, which fell under the invasion of Unova’s army. Early encounters with the horrors of war, witnessing the Unova army’s enhanced Lucario soldiers committing mass murder in his village, shaped his views on strength and evolution. As he watched his parents perish, a deep-seated hatred for Pokémon, especially those used as weapons, took root within him.

Convinced that the key to preventing future atrocities was to advance human evolution to the point where dependency on Pokémon would be a thing of the past, Adenis devoted his life to this singular goal. His path eventually led him to a secretive pact with Deoxys—an agreement that would see New Genesis serve as the vanguard for a supposed upcoming invasion by the Deoxys species. Seizing this opportunity, Adenis vowed to enforce human evolution by spreading Deoxys’ virus to create an evolved form of humanity.

To achieve his vision, Adenis orchestrated grand plans of chaos. With access to advanced technology, he rent open Ultra Wormholes, bringing powerful Guardian Pokémon to Earth, adding to the destabilization. By forming ties with entities such as Guanis, a spying Deoxys disguised as his adoptive daughter, Adenis further entrenched himself in an intricate web of deception and manipulation.

Adenis’s history is a story of a man who transmuted his suffering and loss into a desire for power and control. The trauma of his past, the burning need for revenge, and the unwavering pursuit of a perverted ideal of evolution, forged him into the unyielding leader of New Genesis.

Personality:
Adenis is multilayered—on the surface, he possesses a magnetic charisma that draws followers into New Genesis, inspiring fanatical devotion. He is articulate, immaculately presenting himself as the visionary leader poised to catapult humanity into a new era. Despite the brutal childhood that honed his instincts for survival, Adenis successfully conceals his true nature beneath a veneer of sophistication and apparent benevolence.

Beneath this well-curated exterior lies a cunning, ruthless tactician. Adenis wields his intelligent and strategic mind like a weapon, manipulating his followers and enemies alike. He is a man who believes that the ends justify the means, viewing violence and deception as necessary tools to achieve his grandiose objectives. Adenis can be cold-hearted and merciless, especially against those who would oppose or hinder his cause.

However, Adenis’s psyche is disturbed, rooted in the profound psychological turmoil of his past. His vision of forcibly advancing human evolution emerges as a twisted solution to his childhood traumas and a consuming desire for revenge against the entities he perceives as responsible for mankind’s suffering. His deep-seated hatred of Pokémon, particularly those exploited as tools of war, drives his every action.

Adenis’s complex character is further complicated by his conflicted emotions regarding his supposed daughter Guanis, whom he uses as a pawn in his schemes. While he understands the need for allies, even among the species he despises, his interactions with Guanis are devoid of genuine affection, serving as a reminder of his ultimate goals.

Adenis walks a fine line between visionary and madman, constantly battling the darker urges that threaten to consume him. His relentless quest for power, paired with a willingness to sacrifice anything or anyone for his cause, makes him both a formidable leader and a dangerous enemy. He is a deeply flawed individual, whose tortured soul mirrors his fractured world view—a world where humanity must rise, evolved and supreme, over the ashes of a decrepit society he is all too willing to burn down.


Name: Guanis

Gender: Female 

Age: 13 (Appears 13, but is an ancient Deoxys)

Guanis, the Infiltrator Deoxys

Appearance:
Guanis presents herself as an enigmatic and disciplined young teen with the ethereal beauty and poise that hide her true nature. Her silver hair cascades like a moonlit waterfall, framing a face of cold perfection. The violet eyes of Guanis carry a depth one could easily get lost within, hiding centuries of knowledge and inscrutable intent.

Her human disguise is impeccable: a well-fitted school uniform that gives nothing away, a neatness and simplicity to her attire that allow her to fade into the background when necessary. But those same eyes, if observed closely, are windows into an alien psyche, shimmering with a hidden gleam of otherworldly calculation and superiority.

Backstory:
Guanis’s origin story as one of the most formidable Deoxys is not one of peace or serenity but of millennia-long strife and an incessant drive for dominance. Her existence, predating known civilizations, is steeped in the ruthless survival of the fittest credo of her species. Guanis earned her might, consuming countless others of her kind, assuming their strength and knowledge. Each fallen Deoxys her highly developed psychic appendage absorbed increased her power twofold, elevating Guanis to unmatched heights in the Deoxys hierarchy.

Her consciousness has evolved in tandem with her strength, becoming so intricate that she could slip into disguises within other species’ societies and pass undetected. Guanis’s role became one of a shadowy observer, walking among those marked for conquest, learning their strengths and weaknesses to ensure when the invasion came, it would be swift and decisive.

The infiltration of Earth’s institutions - the Psychic Society and the New Genesis organization - was merely a continuation of Guanis’s mission. This dual allegiance allows her to manipulate human endeavors for her gain, subtly guiding their discoveries and technologies to quell any potential resistance Earth might muster while paving the way for the Deoxys hegemony.

Personality:
Beneath her facade, Guanis is a being of multifaceted complexity. With the intellect of a chess grandmaster, she plays the long game, orchestrating events on Earth to lay the groundwork for her species’ inevitable invasion. She possesses poise and a stoic demeanor that seldom fracture, revealing the predatory nature beneath.

Guanis’s view of humanity is one of contempt concealed by a veneer of curiosity. Her interactions with humans are experiments—ways to study reactions and collect valuable data without revealing herself. While her presence among humans is meticulous and her adaptation to their customs flawless, she can’t help but view them as experiments, unworthy of the freedom they so carelessly enjoy.

Invested in uncovering weaknesses, Guanis maintains a strict discipline not to interfere overtly with human lives outside her agenda. She is extremely patient, ready to wait for decades or even centuries for the right moment to strike. But her predatory instinct to exploit the slightest vulnerability when presented has not dulled with age.

Inside, a slow-burning rage against the Deoxys renegade Cytos and his lineage motivates her every move. She craves vengeance for the betrayal she perceives in Cytos’s abandonment of their collective mission — a betrayal marked in her mind as an affront to the Deoxys supremacy she upholds.

In encounters with the offspring of Cytos, young Pyrimis, Guanis’s instincts war against her reasoning. Her radiating presence of power is restrained, for now, biding until she can deliver both punishment and warning to any other potential traitors to her species’ intrinsic dominion.

Guanis’s integration into human society only emphasizes her alienness. She is an observer always on the periphery, a spy who waits for the unraveling of a species she considers doomed. But her psychic capabilities and the strength she draws from her Deoxys heritage instill a fear that makes even her unwitting human compatriots uneasy, sensing an undercurrent of threat in her unnervingly perfect mimicry.

Guanis’s dedication to her mission challenges her understanding of unity and dominion. As she surrounds herself with humans each day and weaves herself more intricately into their lives, she finds the need to constantly reaffirm her resolve. Her commitment never falters, yet the time spent among humans inevitably forges connections she must then strive to sever mentally.

Guanis holds within her psychic grasp the power to sway the fate of planets. Her every step is measured, her every word calculated, her silence pregnant with meanings humans can scarcely understand. Guanis harbors not just the cold logic of conquest but a relentless drive to prove her evolutionary supremacy and worthiness as heir to Earth, showcasing the might of Deoxys for all to witness, in awe or terror.

Name: Mahina

Gender: Female  

Age: 13

Appearance:  
Mahina is an athletic girl with lightly tanned skin that reflects her Alolan heritage. She has long, dark brown hair typically kept back from her lively hazel eyes with a flower clip. Mahina dresses comfortably for ease of movement but favors bright, cheerful colors reflecting her warm personality. She is rarely without her flower-patterned backpack holding her treasured Pokéballs and battle essentials. Overall, Mahina’s energetic appearance mirrors her passionate spirit.

Backstory:
Mahina comes from a distinguished lineage of remarkable women. Her mother Selene made history as Alola's first ever Pokémon League Champion before joining the Ultra Recon Squad conservation group. Her father Hau continued the family legacy by succeeding his grandfather Hala as Melemele Island’s Kahuna.

Due to Selene’s frequent exposure to Ultra Space, Mahina was born with powerful psychic abilities. To help control her surging psychic energies from harming Mahina or others, she underwent transplantation of Gardevoir cells as an infant. This allowed regulation of her psionic powers but also placed an innate limit on her abilities. 

Driven to follow in her family’s footsteps, Mahina joined the Psionic Society at a young age to train her psychic talents. However, the existence of Gardevoir DNA in her body has stymied her progression, unable to push her powers beyond a bottleneck. Mahina works tirelessly but cannot seem to overcome this barrier, leaving her feeling inadequate next to her high-achieving kin. Unbeknownst to her, transcending this genetic block may unlock staggering new heights of power.

Personality:  
Mahina bubbles over with enthusiasm and passion for life. She firmly believes in supporting those in need and will boldly stand against injustice or cruelty. This strong moral compass stems from Mahina's deeply caring spirit beneath her cheery exterior. 

While quite extroverted and openly expressive, Mahina is sensitive and takes failures firmly to heart. Her stalled psychic development despite tireless training has impacted her confidence. However, she tackles every challenge with determined resilience, hoping to overcome her limitations.

Overall, Mahina’s energetic and affectionate nature makes her a beloved friend. Her positivity and selfless courage light up those around her. Mahina lives life to the fullest while aiming to touch as many hearts as possible on her personal journey of growth.

Name: Helia

Gender: Female  

Age: 10

Appearance:  
Helia is a petite girl with fair skin, bright blonde hair that falls just past her shoulders, and large blue eyes that exude innocence. She tends to dress simply, favoring pastel colors and practical shoes suited for play. Helia's small stature and soft features give her a delicate appearance. However, when she smiles, her eyes light up with enthusiasm.

Backstory:
Helia was born with a life-threatening illness. To save her baby's life, Helia's mother Professor Lillie took a great risk - she transplanted a shard of the extra-dimensional Pokémon Necrozma into Helia. This imbued Helia with the energy needed to survive and grow.

As Helia gets older, the dormant powers of Necrozma within her are gradually awakening. However, Helia remains unaware of her hidden strengths, believing herself to be shy and frail. In truth, she possesses the ability to open Ultra Wormholes and wield the light-manipulating might of Ultra Necrozma. But this power still sleeps, waiting to be unleashed.

Helia grew up in Kanto as the daughter of Professor Lillie and Elio. She has always admired her confident cousin Mahina and dreams of becoming a great Pokémon Trainer someday too. Though timid, Helia's kind heart yearns to help others. Joining Mahina in Alola, she hopes to gain courage and make true friends. Little does she know the great allies her power could attract, for good or evil.

Personality:  
Helia is a kindhearted and gentle soul who sees the good in everyone. She is soft-spoken and introverted, often preferring her own company or that of a few close friends. Helia has a vivid imagination and whimsical outlook on life.

While shy around new people, once comfortable Helia is playful and affectionate. She dreams of going on exciting adventures with Pokémon by her side. However, Helia's sensitive nature makes it difficult for her to stand up to confrontation or cruelty from others. She relies heavily on Mahina's protection from bullies, lacking confidence in her own hidden strength. Above all, Helia desires to find the courage within herself to become the brave trainer she dreams of being.

Pokemon Team：
1.	Khlap


Name: Shmaya

Gender: Female 

Age: 13 (Appears 13, but is an ancient and timeless being)

Appearance:
Shmaya appears as a young teen girl with an air of wisdom beyond her years. She has long, flowing green hair the color of emeralds that falls to her waist. Her eyes are a piercing gold, and her irises bear the mystical delta symbol of Rayquaza. Shmaya moves with an elegant grace, exuding a powerful yet benevolent presence.

As the student council president, Shmaya dresses neatly in her school uniform. The crisp white blouse and pleated skirt perfectly match her polished black shoes. A green ribbon accents her uniform, hinting at her connection to Rayquaza. Shmaya carries herself with poise and dignity, befitting her role as a leader.

Backstory:  
Shmaya is the human incarnation of Rayquaza, the legendary Pokémon charged with protecting the Earth. As guardian of the skies, Rayquaza sensed a great disturbance rippling across the solar system. Foreseeing a crisis that threatened the Earth, Rayquaza took on the form of a young teen girl named Shmaya.

Blending into human society, Shmaya founded the Psychic Society to identify and train gifted humans. She recognized fellow disguised Pokémon like Pyrimis and Guanis immediately. Though appearing benign, Shmaya has secretly been battling against Guanis while nurturing Pyrimis' potential.

Shmaya walks amongst humans, ever vigilant for threats to her beloved planet. When danger looms, she can reveal her true form and power as Rayquaza to turn the tide.

Personality:
